title: Global Reforestation Tracker Update — Apr 29, 2026
summary: Kenya announced a historic 24-hour mangrove restoration drive at Mombasa's Tudor Creek, led by a Guinness World Record holder and coordinated under the national 15 Billion Tree Growing Programme. FIFA's World Cup 2026 urban forest initiative with the Arbor Day Foundation surpassed 250,000 trees planted across all 16 North American host cities since launching in 2025. Orange County, Florida launched 'OC Loves Trees' on Arbor Day with developer-fee-funded planting of native trees, while Minnesota's DNR announced a spring campaign to plant nearly 2 million seedlings on state forest land. Amazon deforestation 2025 trend data (August 2025–January 2026: 1,325 km²) continues to point toward a second consecutive historic low under Brazil's Lula government.

title: Global Reforestation & Rewilding Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: The Minnesota Department of Natural Resources (DNR) announced on April 26, 2026, that it would plant nearly 2 million tree seedlings on DNR-managed forest land across the state in spring 2026.

title: Global Reforestation & Rewilding Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: FIFA Launches Tree Planting Initiative Across All 16 World Cup 2026 Host Cities on Earth Day.

title: Global Reforestation Tracker Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: An NPR investigation found only 13% of $19 billion pledged for the Great Green Wall has been disbursed, exposing a critical financing gap as the 2030 deadline approaches. The REMAR project launched in the Eastern Caribbean with €5.5 million to restore mangroves across five territories under a community-centred, hydrological-first approach. India's Odisha state reported a 16.6% decade-long growth in mangrove cover, with the Bhitarkanika 'fishbone channel' restoration model gaining international recognition. Earth Day 2026, themed 'Our Power, Our Planet,' is mobilizing 10,000+ global events including a Guinness World Record tree-planting attempt in Kenya.
